name: Diagrams (Infrastructure as Code for Cloud Architecture Diagrams)

auth.notes: Local library; Graphviz must be installed on system

performance.notes: Graphviz rendering time; proportional to diagram complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Python DSL for cloud architecture diagrams using official cloud icons. from diagrams import Diagram, Cluster, Edge. from diagrams.aws.compute import EC2, ECS, Lambda. from diagrams.aws.database import RDS, ElastiCache. from diagrams.aws.network import ELB, CloudFront. with Diagram('Web Service', show=False, filename='diagram'): lb = ELB('load balancer'). ec2 = EC2('web'). db = RDS('database'). lb >> ec2 >> db. Providers: AWS, GCP, Azure, K8s, generic, on-premise. Cluster() for grouping. Edge() for custom connections. Outputs: PNG, JPG, SVG, DOT.

gotchas: Requires Graphviz system package: brew install graphviz / apt install graphviz
Layout control is limited — Graphviz auto-layouts can be suboptimal for complex diagrams
Icons may become outdated as cloud providers update service names/icons
No interactive/web output — static images only
best_when: Generating cloud architecture diagrams programmatically from code
Documentation pipelines producing diagrams from infrastructure code
When Diagram-as-Code approach is preferred over drag-and-drop tools
avoid_when: Interactive or web-based diagrams (use Mermaid or DrawIO)
Complex custom layouts requiring manual positioning
When Graphviz system dependency is not installable
summary: Diagrams is a Python DSL for cloud architecture diagrams using official AWS/GCP/Azure icons via Graphviz. with Diagram('Web Service'): ELB('lb') >> EC2('web') >> RDS('db'). Cluster() for grouping. Outputs PNG/SVG/DOT. Requires graphviz system package. MIT by mingrammer. Perfect for Diagram-as-Code in documentation pipelines and CI/CD. Static images only — use Mermaid for interactive/web.
